meaning: even if / even granting that (concession — the conclusion remains unchanged)

exercises.example_sentence: お金があったとしても、それは買わない。
exercises.translation: Even if I had the money, I would not buy that.
wrong_choice_explanations: This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜としても' (even if / even granting that (concession — the conclusion remains unchanged)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜としても' (even if / even granting that (concession — the conclusion remains unchanged)) needed in this context.
This pattern does not convey the same meaning as '〜としても' (even if / even granting that (concession — the conclusion remains unchanged)) needed in this context.

exercises.example_sentence: 時間があったとしても、行きたくない。
exercises.translation: Even if I had time, I would not want to go.
wrong_choice_explanations: たにしても has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where としても is required.
ても has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where としても is required.
たとしたら has a different grammatical function and doesn't fit the context of this sentence where としても is required.

exercises.example_sentence: 彼が謝ったとしても、許さない。
exercises.translation: Even if he apologized, I would not forgive him.
wrong_choice_explanations: たにしても has a different grammatical function, but 〜としても is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.
ても even if / no matter, but 〜としても is needed to express the specific meaning required in this sentence.
たとしたら conditional if / when, but 〜としても has a specific meaning, which is what this sentence requires.

exercises.example_sentence: 証拠があったとしても、信じない。
exercises.translation: Even if there were evidence, I would not believe it.
wrong_choice_explanations: たにしても has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
ても has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.
たとしたら has a different grammatical function that doesn't fit this sentence context.

exercises.example_sentence: 説得されたとしても、考えは変わらない。
exercises.translation: Even if convinced, my opinion would not change.
wrong_choice_explanations: 〜たにしても is past tense form; not appropriate here.
〜ても is simple concession; doesn't have formal tone.
〜たとしたら is hypothetical; doesn't convey concession.
